 {"id":"bd-1nz8","title":"Input path parity (runtime vs InputParser)","description":"Problem: TerminalSession uses crossterm events while InputParser handles raw bytes. Semantics differ (kitty keyboard, BackTab or Null or Backspace, mouse drag or scroll and modifier mapping), leading to inconsistent model updates across modes.

Scope:
- Define a single set of input semantics and map both paths to it (either route runtime through InputParser or bridge crossterm events to the same Event representation).
- Ensure parity for kitty keyboard sequences, modifiers, BackTab, Null, Backspace, mouse drag and scroll, and any special keys used by the harness.
- Preserve ordering and repeat behavior; document differences that cannot be reconciled.

Testing:
- Unit tests in ftui-core that verify parity for keycodes, modifiers, and mouse semantics.
- E2E test driving both runtime and input-parser modes and comparing logged events (JSON summary and PTY capture).
- Negative tests for malformed or partial sequences to ensure safe handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Input semantics match across input paths for all supported keys and mouse events.
- Kitty keyboard events are preserved end-to-end.
- Tests demonstrate parity and safe handling.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:28:45.207144262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T20:41:25.506143543Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:41:25.506125028Z","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive input parity tests (31 tests) verifying consistency between InputParser and crossterm event mapping paths","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1oa1y","title":"Legend/Footnote Layout Primitives","description":"Background
- Link footnotes/legends need consistent, deterministic layout across sizes.
- Ad-hoc legend placement risks overlap with diagrams and breaks scaling.

Scope
- Define a generic legend layout block: placement (below/right), width allocation, and max height rules.
- Compute line wrapping and truncation for legend entries.
- Reserve and expose a legend region to the renderer without shifting diagram bounds unpredictably.
- Provide deterministic layout decisions based on size + config.
- Emit legend metrics (legend_mode, legend_height, legend_lines) for evidence logs.

Considerations
- Must be deterministic; no dynamic probing.
- Must integrate cleanly with scale tiers and diagram bounds.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for legend sizing, wrap, truncation, and placement.
- Snapshot tests for footnote legend on/off at multiple sizes.
- PTY E2E logs include legend_height and legend_mode.

Success Criteria
- Legend/footnote regions never overlap diagram content.
- Legend layout is stable across runs and sizes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueOtter","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:48:18.908462417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:42:54.517071134Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:42:54.517007424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oa1y","depends_on_id":"bd-25df9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:48:23.951514551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1oa1y","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:48:23.711756106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1oyeg","title":"Parser: DA/DSR device queries + character set designation (G0/G1) + single shifts","description":"## Background
The VT parser handles 56 action variants covering most core terminal sequences, but lacks several important features needed for application compatibility.

## Gaps to Fill

### Device Attribute / Status Reports (Tier 1 — critical handshake)
- DA1 (CSI c / CSI 0 c): Primary device attributes — many CLI programs send this as a startup handshake
- DA2 (CSI > c / CSI > 0 c): Secondary device attributes (terminal type/version)
- DSR (CSI 5 n): Device status report — "are you OK?" query
- CPR (CSI 6 n): Cursor position report request

### Character Set Designation (Tier 1 — affects rendering)
- G0 designate (ESC ( B / ESC ( 0): ASCII / DEC Special Graphics
- G1 designate (ESC ) B / ESC ) 0): ASCII / DEC Special Graphics
- These are used by ncurses, vim, tmux for box-drawing characters

### Single Shift (Tier 2 — completeness)
- SS2 (ESC N): Single shift to G2
- SS3 (ESC O): Single shift to G3

## Deliverables
- New Action variants for DA1, DA2, DSR, CPR, DesignateCharset, SingleShift
- Parser dispatch for all new sequences
- Unit tests for each new sequence
- Conformance fixtures for key sequences
- Wire new actions in test harnesses (no-op in harness is fine)

## Testing
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2026-02-09T04:09:19.791783954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:17:52.264271402Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T04:17:47.890125157Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1048,"issue_id":"bd-1oyeg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: 8 new Action variants (DeviceAttributes, DeviceAttributesSecondary, DeviceStatusReport, CursorPositionReport, DesignateCharset, SingleShift2, SingleShift3), CSI dispatch for c/>c/n, ESC dispatch for (/)/*//+/N/O, EscCharset state, reply engine integration, 15 unit tests, 3 conformance fixtures. Changes landed in 500b69db.","created_at":"2026-02-09T04:17:52Z"}]}
